Operation 'unsubscribeWebhook' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. It doesn't specify what input is required (subscription ID), what the response contains, or the side effect (cessation of notifications). The improved description clarifies the required input from WebhookUnsubscribeRequest schema, explains the response behavior, and explicitly states the side effect of stopping notifications, making it more actionable for AI agents.

data.current_description: Removes a webhook subscription to stop receiving notifications.
data.description_suggestion: Removes an active webhook subscription to stop receiving event notifications. Requires the webhook subscription ID in the request body. Returns a confirmation response indicating successful unsubscription. After unsubscription, no further event notifications will be sent to the associated webhook endpoint.
data.reasoning: The current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. It doesn't specify what input is required (subscription ID), what the response contains, or the side effect (cessation of notifications). The improved description clarifies the required input from WebhookUnsubscribeRequest schema, explains the response behavior, and explicitly states the side effect of stopping notifications, making it more actionable for AI agents.

data.current_description: Retrieves a paginated list of created waivers with optional filtering capabilities.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of created waivers with optional filtering and sorting capabilities. Supports filtering by folder, status, and date range. Returns waiver summaries with pagination metadata. Use this operation to browse available waivers or search for specific waivers matching your criteria.

data.current_description: Creates a webhook subscription to receive notifications for specified events.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a webhook subscription to receive event notifications at a specified endpoint. Requires the webhook URL and event types to subscribe to in the request body. Returns a subscription object containing the subscription ID, URL, event types, and creation timestamp. The subscription becomes active immediately and will begin sending notifications for matching events. Multiple subscriptions to the same URL are allowed.
